stemHtml: In Foehn conditions, moist air ascends the windward side of a mountain, losing moisture as it rises. As it descends on the leeward side, changes in temperature and pressure occur. Which statement best describes these changes on the leeward side?

options.a: The leeward side is cooler, because the air expands and cools as it descends with no moisture.
options.b: The leeward side is warmer, because the air warms at the DALR due to compression as pressure increases during descent.
options.c: The leeward side is cooler, because the air descends rapidly and loses pressure.
options.d: The leeward side is warmer, because the descending air retains moisture and warms at the SALR.
